# Load the file
file_path = '/mnt/data/file-1Vvaj61hkAYh4hxkoUFKKv-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(file_path, 'rb') as f:
    data = f.read()
# Let's decode as utf-8 and print content
text = data.decode('utf-8')
print(text)

# locate that paraphrase
start = text.find("حين يقول")
snippet = text[start:start+200]
print(snippet)

# find substring
pos_colon = text.find(":", start)
pos_qm = text.find("؟", pos_colon)
sub = text[pos_colon+1:pos_qm]
print(repr(sub))

# find reference [2] snippet
ref2_start = text.find("[2]")
print(ref2_start)
snippet2 = text[ref2_start:ref2_start+200]
print(snippet2)

# isolate ref2 Matn
pos_colon2 = text.find(":", ref2_start+50)  # find colon after [2] url
pos_qm2 = text.find("؟", pos_colon2)
sub2 = text[pos_colon2+1:pos_qm2]
print(repr(sub2))

# find reference [3]
ref3_start = text.find("[3]")
print(ref3_start)
snippet3 = text[ref3_start:ref3_start+500]
print(snippet3)

# get content between parentheses for first
start1 = snippet3.find("(")
end1 = snippet3.find(")", start1)
matn1 = snippet3[start1+1:end1]
print(repr(matn1))
# matn1 includes ellipsis and comma. Let's show.
print(matn1)

# find second parentheses
start2 = snippet3.find("(", end1+1)
end2 = snippet3.find(")", start2)
matn2_full = snippet3[start2+1:end2]
print(repr(matn2_full))
print(matn2_full)

# find reference [4]
ref4_start = text.find("[4]")
print(ref4_start)
snippet4 = text[ref4_start:ref4_start+200]
print(snippet4)

# isolate phrase
pos_colon4 = text.find(":", ref4_start)
pos_qm4 = text.find("؟", pos_colon4)
sub4 = text[pos_colon4+1:pos_qm4]
print(repr(sub4))
print(sub4)

# Let's gather spans. Define spans texts and find all occurrences considering approximate positions.

spans = []
# define span texts
span_texts = []

# Span1
st1 = "ءَأَنتَ ‌قُلۡتَ ‌لِلنَّاسِ ٱتَّخِذُونِي وَأُمِّيَ إِلَٰهَيۡنِ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ"
span_texts.append(("span1", st1))

# Span2
st2 = "مَا قُلْتُ لَهُمْ إِلَّا مَا أَمَرْتَنِي بِهِ أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبَّكُمْ وَكُنتُ عَلَيْهِمْ شَهِيدًا مَا دُمْتُ فِيهِمْ فَلَمَّا تَوَفَّيْتَنِي كُنتَ أَنْتَ الرَّقِيبَ عَلَيْهِمْ وَأَنْتَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ شَهِيدٌ"
span_texts.append(("span2", st2))

# Span3
st3 = "وَكَذَلِكَ نُرِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ مَلَكُوتَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأَرْضِ وَلِيَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُوقِنِينَ * فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ رَأَى كَوْكَبًا قَالَ هَذَا رَبِّي فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَا أُحِبُّ الْآفِلِينَ * فَلَمَّا رَأَى الْقَمَرَ بَاغِرًا قَالَ هَذَا رَبِّي فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قَالَ لَئِن لَّمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ * فَلَمَّا رَأَى الشَّمْسَ بَاغِرًا قَالَ هَذَا رَبِّي هَذَا أَكْبَرُ فَلَمَّا أَفَلَتْ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِّمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ * إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِي لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالأَرْضَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ"
span_texts.append(("span3", st3))

# Span4
st4 = "صَيِّروني وأمي معبودَيْن من دون الله"
span_texts.append(("span4", st4))

# Span5
st5 = "ءَأَنتَ ‌قُلۡتَ ‌لِلنَّاسِ ٱتَّخِذُونِي وَأُمِّيَ إِلَٰهَيۡنِ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ"
span_texts.append(("span5", st5))

# Span6 identical to 5
st6 = st5
span_texts.append(("span6", st6))

# Span7
st7 = "أأنتَ قلت للناس اجعلوني وأمي معبودين من دون الله"
span_texts.append(("span7", st7))

# Span labels correspond to logical occurrences
# Now find occurrences in text around expected positions

results = []
for label, txt in span_texts:
    # find all occurrences
    idx = text.find(txt)
    print(label, 'first idx', idx)
    if idx == -1:
        # maybe need to replace multiple spaces or NBSP? Let's try substring ignoring zero width space \u200c
        txt_simple = txt.replace("\u200c", "")
        idx = text.find(txt_simple)
        print(label, 'simple idx', idx)
        if idx == -1:
            raise ValueError(f"{label} not found")
        else:
            txt = txt_simple
    start = idx
    end = start + len(txt)
    # confirm
    assert text[start:end] == txt, f"{label} mismatch"
    results.append({'label': label, 'start': start, 'end': end, 'text': txt})
results

# extract sub4 properly and print repr
start4 = text.find("[4]")
pos_colon4 = text.find(":", start4)
pos_qm4 = text.find("؟", pos_colon4)
sub4 = text[pos_colon4+1:pos_qm4]
# trimmed version
trim = sub4[sub4.find("أأنت"):]
trim = trim.strip()
print(repr(trim))

# redefine st7
st7 = trim
idx7 = text.find(st7)
print(idx7, repr(text[idx7:idx7+len(st7)]))

# Find all occurrences of st5
txt5 = st5
occ = []
start_search = 0
while True:
    idx = text.find(txt5, start_search)
    if idx == -1:
        break
    occ.append(idx)
    start_search = idx+1
occ

for idx in occ:
    print(idx, text[idx-10:idx+len(txt5)+10])
print(text[3050:3150])
print(text[3000:3400])
print(text[3830:3950])

# Prepare JSON citation objects
citations = []
# For st1 occurrences
txt1 = span_texts[0][1]  # st1
for idx in occ:
    # verify that substring matches
    sub = text[idx:idx+len(txt1)]
    # trim trailing ellipsis for the one with ellipsis?
    # Actually in occ, the one at 2230 has ellipsis in text: "...". The substring txt1 includes no ellipsis. At 2230, there is "...". So text[2230:2230+len(txt1)] should equal txt1? Actually at 2230, it's "ءَأَنتَ...ٱللَّهِ". It is before the "...". Let's check.
    snippet = text[2230:2230+len(txt1)+3]
    print(snippet[:len(txt1)], '==', txt1)

# Build citations
citations = []
# st1 occurrences
txt1 = st1
for idx in occ:
    citations.append({
        'span_start': idx,
        'span_end': idx + len(txt1),
        'span_text': txt1,
        'citation_type': 'Aya'
    })

# st2
idx2 = text.find(st2)
citations.append({
    'span_start': idx2,
    'span_end': idx2 + len(st2),
    'span_text': st2,
    'citation_type': 'Aya'
})

# st3
idx3 = text.find(st3)
citations.append({
    'span_start': idx3,
    'span_end': idx3 + len(st3),
    'span_text': st3,
    'citation_type': 'Aya'
})

# st4
idx4 = text.find(st4)
citations.append({
    'span_start': idx4,
    'span_end': idx4 + len(st4),
    'span_text': st4,
    'citation_type': 'Aya'
})

# st7
idx7 = text.find(st7)
citations.append({
    'span_start': idx7,
    'span_end': idx7 + len(st7),
    'span_text': st7,
    'citation_type': 'Aya'
})

len(citations)

for c in citations:
    print(c)
